scope: Compact agent-facing reference for MoonCat hue/color fields and adjacent color-model context. This file records source-reconciled guidance, ADR-defined hue bucket ranges, aggregate observations from local reference snapshots, and curated character-cat context; it is not a per-cat trait dataset, exact rendering palette, contract ABI, parser source import, or generated image dataset.

provenance.notes: ADR 0017 is the strongest reviewed current-maintainer technical source for hue truncation, parser canonicality, and human-friendly hue bucket boundaries. Observed hue names, hueInt ranges, pale counts, and genesis coverage are aggregate-only observations from the local mooncat_traits.json reference snapshot. Character-cat hue ranges remain community-curated narrative/filter data. Parser and on-chain helper names are compact surface evidence only; exact palette values, generated image output, source internals, and per-cat mappings are not imported.

claims: ADR 0017 defines current-maintainer technical guidance: original JavaScript mooncatparser behavior, including RGBToHSL output, is canonical for true MoonCat color.
ADR 0017 says hue values are truncated to integer values before use.
ADR 0017 defines twelve human-friendly HLS/HSL wheel labels as 30-degree buckets with lower-exclusive and upper-inclusive boundaries.
The local mooncat_traits.json snapshot aggregate aligns with those ADR bucket boundaries for observed non-special hueName/hueInt ranges.
The local parser snapshot supports HSL-based hue extraction and palette derivation at a source-surface level, but this file does not import exact parser formulas or output palettes.
MoonCatColors contract surfaces and the MoonCats On-Chain Guide support an 8-color / 24-number color output shape, but this file does not define exact color semantics or palette values.
notPromoted: No per-cat color mappings are imported.
No exact RGB or hex palettes are imported.
No full parser source, ABI, contract source, image output, or generated data is imported.
Pale rendering behavior remains guarded because reviewed strong sources confirm the pale field and high-confidence explanation, but exact rendering semantics still need parser/contract confirmation.

sourceBackedClaims.claim: ADR 0017 says MoonCatRescue projects shall treat original JavaScript mooncatparser behavior, including RGBToHSL output, as canonical for true MoonCat color.
limitations: This file does not import parser source internals or edge-case overrides.

sourceBackedClaims.claim: ADR 0017 says hue values are truncated to integer values before use.
limitations: Floating-point edge cases can require implementation-specific care; this file does not list per-cat overrides.

sourceBackedClaims.claim: Observed hueName/hueInt aggregate ranges in the local trait snapshot match ADR-defined integer bucket ranges for non-special hue names.
limitations: The aggregate snapshot is not a per-cat lookup and does not prove rendering output.

sourceBackedClaims.claim: Reviewed contract and library surfaces support colorsOf returning 24 uint8 values, and the MoonCats On-Chain Guide explains that as RGB values for 8 colors.
limitations: Exact color ordering, palette semantics, and edge-case mapColors values should be reviewed in contract/source internals before canonical import.

notes: ADR 0017 defines labels as equal 30-degree HLS/HSL wheel divisions.
ADR 0017 says hue values are truncated to integers before use.
Observed snapshot ranges below reconcile those ADR boundaries with aggregate hueName/hueInt values.

buckets.label: sky blue
buckets.notes: ADR uses the display label Sky Blue; the observed local trait snapshot uses skyblue as the compact field value.

reconciliationNotes: The observed non-special hueName ranges already match ADR integer buckets, including the red wrap over zero.
Black and white are observed snapshot labels with non-degree hueInt values; they are not ADR 30-degree hue buckets.

limitations: The full 25,440-row source dataset is not imported into this curated data file.
Counts and ranges are aggregate snapshot observations, not a guarantee of current API or on-chain behavior.
hueInt values for black and white are recorded as observed special numeric values, not as hue degrees.

hueName.description: Name-like color bucket observed on every row in the local mooncat_traits.json snapshot.
doNotInfer: Do not treat this file as a complete per-cat hueName lookup.
Do not infer pale, genesis, or rendering palette from hueName alone.

hueInt.description: Numeric color value observed on every row in the local mooncat_traits.json snapshot.
hueInt.degreeLikeRange: Most non-special hue names use observed values in 0-359 degree-like ranges.
doNotInfer: Do not convert hueInt directly to hex colors from this file.
Do not treat black=1000 or white=2000 as hue degrees.

pale.description: Boolean trait metadata field observed on every row in the local mooncat_traits.json snapshot.
doNotInfer: Do not infer pale from hueName.
Do not apply a rendering adjustment unless a separate rendering source is being used.

genesis.description: Optional boolean field observed as true on 96 rows in the local mooncat_traits.json snapshot.
doNotInfer: Do not use this file as a genesis catId list.
Use data/mooncat-types.json and data/special-cats.json for curated genesis context.

observedHueNames.notes: Observed hueInt is a non-degree special value in the local snapshot.
Observed values cross the 359-to-0 boundary; do not interpret min/max as a continuous full-circle range.
Observed hueInt is a non-degree special value in the local snapshot.

notes: ADR-defined hue bucket boundaries are promoted as current-maintainer technical guidance in hueBucketModel.
The observed hueName inventory remains aggregate snapshot evidence and is not a per-cat lookup.
Exact rendering palettes, hex values, edge-color tidbits, and per-cat overrides remain out of scope for this file.

observed.notes: Observed as a hueName with a non-degree hueInt value in the local snapshot.
Observed as a hueName with a non-degree hueInt value in the local snapshot.
Observed as optional genesis=true rows in the local snapshot. Use curated type/special-cat files for genesis context instead of treating this file as a membership list.

limitations: This section does not define official special coat categories.
This section does not provide catId membership lists.

notes: Use character-cat hue ranges only as curated narrative/filter data. Do not treat them as official coat traits.
Some character-cat hue ranges wrap over zero and should use wrapsZero: true rather than being read as simple min-to-max intervals.

rules.rule: Treat pale as a separate boolean metadata field when it is present.
Do not infer pale from hueName alone.
Do not apply a pale rendering adjustment from this file; keep exact rendering behavior tied to a focused parser or contract-source review.

highConfidenceExplanation.summary: The attributed Discord explanation says pale MoonCats use the derived palette differently from standard MoonCats.
limitations: No raw Discord export or URL was reviewed.
The exact pale rendering rule is not promoted as canonical here.

paleHandling.notes: Observed pale is source-backed as a trait field; exact pale rendering behavior remains unresolved in this compact model.

surfaceClaims: The parser snapshot contains RGBToHSL, HSLToRGB, RGBToHex, and derivePalette helpers.
The parser snapshot uses RGBToHSL inside derivePalette and uses HSLToRGB for palette entries.
The parser snapshot has special genesis color arrays before falling back to derivePalette for non-genesis parsing.
notes: ADR 0017 promotes original JavaScript mooncatparser behavior as canonical for true MoonCat color.
This file does not copy parser source code, palette constants, or generated image output.
Use docs/color-math.md for the repo's current circular hue-distance method note.

paletteDerivationStatus.summary: Source review supports that MoonCat color rendering derives palettes from RGB/HSL-style helper logic, but this compact file does not define the full palette formula or exact output colors.
promotedSurfaceFacts: Original parser behavior is the current-maintainer canonical reference for true color.
Local parser snapshot contains derivePalette and HSL conversion helper surfaces.
The on-chain guide describes a single embedded color code being expanded into image and accessory colors.
notPromoted: Exact lightness values or hue-shift constants as reusable formulas.

promotedSurfaceFacts: The local trait snapshot observes 96 genesis=true rows, 48 black hueName rows, and 48 white hueName rows.
The local parser snapshot contains a genesis branch with special color arrays.
Reddit explanation supports black/white visual categorization as maintainer/community context.
limitations: This file does not provide genesis catId membership.
Black and white are observed special non-degree values, not ADR hue-wheel buckets.
Genesis rendering details are not fully canonicalized here.
This file does not import ABI JSON, contract source internals, color values, accessory palettes, or edge-case mapColors data.
The 8-color semantic order is article-supported context and should be verified against contract/source internals before exact canonical use.

circularHueDistance.notes: Useful comparison method from docs/color-math.md; canonical hue targets still need sources.

agentUsageNotes: Use hueBucketModel for ADR-defined current-maintainer hue bucket boundaries.
For aggregate observed color buckets, load this file and data/trait-index.json before consulting the full upstream snapshot.
For source provenance and unresolved claim categories, load data/color-source-inventory.json before changing this file.
For character-cat matching context, load data/character-cats.json and data/character-cat-index.json; keep that context separate from official protocol trait claims.
For genesis context, load data/mooncat-types.json and data/special-cats.json; do not use this file as a genesis catId list.
For rendering/parser implementation questions, inspect references/upstream/mooncatrescue/mooncatparser.js directly; this file is only a surface and aggregate index.
Do not mix catId/rescue-order identifier conversion rules into hue interpretation. Identifier conversion is handled by separate identifier and LibMoonCat references.
guardrails: Do not derive per-cat hueName, hueInt, pale status, or palette values from this compact file.
Do not treat black=1000 or white=2000 as hue degrees.
Do not use Reddit or Discord explanations alone for canonical formulas or exact rendering behavior.
Do not copy full parser source, contract ABI/source, generated images, or mooncat_traits.json rows into this file.
If a future source conflicts with ADR 0017 or the observed snapshot ranges, document the conflict with sourceRefs instead of silently overwriting.

unresolvedRenderingQuestions.notes: High-confidence maintainer explanation exists, but exact behavior is not promoted from transcript alone.
ADR supports ignoring pure coat pattern color for clone/littermate visual matching. This file does not generalize that into a complete rendering rule.
The guide and transcript discuss glow/original color context, but exact visual use across all renderers is not canonicalized here.
ADR says mapColors compensates for Solidity/JavaScript math differences; no edge-case mapping values are imported.

limitations: No per-cat rows, catId-to-color mappings, exact hex palettes, or generated image colors are included.
Observed aggregate counts come from a local upstream reference snapshot and are not per-cat lookup data.
ADR-defined hue bucket boundaries are source-backed, but exact rendering palettes and pale rendering behavior remain unresolved in this repository.
Character-cat color ranges are community-curated and incomplete.
todos: Review parser/rendering behavior separately before adding exact palette or hex-color references.
Review MoonCatColors internals before importing mapColors overrides, paletteOf semantics, or exact color ordering.
Document official special non-hue coat categories only after a focused source-backed review.
Keep any future per-cat color import in a separate generated-data pass rather than expanding this compact agent index.
